text.title: Go Concurrency Rules
text.summary: Rules for goroutine lifecycle, channel ownership, context propagation, error capture, and mandatory race detection in Go.

rule.statement: Every `go func()` must have a documented termination path: a channel close, `ctx.Done()`, or a timeout. Add a `runtime.NumGoroutine()` assertion in tests that checks for leaks after `Stop()`.
rule.rationale: Goroutines blocking on a channel that never closes silently consume memory. There is no GC for blocked goroutines.

rule.statement: Always `defer wg.Done()` as the very first statement inside a goroutine body.
rule.rationale: If the goroutine panics before reaching a later `wg.Done()` call, the WaitGroup never decrements and `wg.Wait()` blocks forever.

rule.statement: Add `defer func() { _ = recover() }()` in every long-running worker goroutine to prevent a single panic from crashing the process.
rule.rationale: An unrecovered panic in any goroutine terminates the entire program, not just that goroutine.

rule.statement: One writer, one closer: document channel ownership in a comment at the declaration site. Only the writer closes a channel. Never close from the consumer side.
rule.rationale: Closing a channel from the consumer panics. Closing twice panics. Ownership rules prevent both.

rule.statement: Use directional channel types in function signatures: `<-chan T` for read-only parameters, `chan<- T` for write-only parameters.
rule.rationale: Directional types enforce ownership at compile time; a function receiving `<-chan T` cannot close it.

rule.statement: Size channels deliberately. Document the buffer size rationale in a comment. Never use `make(chan T, 1_000_000)` as a shortcut for unbounded.
rule.rationale: Unbounded buffering is a memory leak; the buffer just delays the OOM. Explicit sizing forces a decision about backpressure.

rule.statement: Every blocking `select` inside a worker must include a `case <-ctx.Done(): return` arm. Never substitute `context.Background()` for a passed context inside a goroutine.
rule.rationale: Omitting `ctx.Done()` means the worker ignores cancellation signals; goroutines leak after the parent operation is cancelled.

rule.statement: Prefer `errgroup.WithContext` from `golang.org/x/sync/errgroup` over hand-rolling `WaitGroup + channel` for fan-out. Use `errgroup.SetLimit` to bound concurrency.
rule.rationale: `errgroup` cancels sibling goroutines on first error and provides a clean error aggregation path without a separate error channel.

rule.statement: Run `go test -race -count=10` for any test that touches channels, goroutines, or shared state. Use `goleak.VerifyTestMain(m)` in test packages that spawn goroutines.
rule.rationale: Race conditions are non-deterministic; single-run tests miss them. `-race -count=10` runs the test 10 times under the race detector, catching most flaky races.

antipattern.description: `for range jobs { job() }` without a `select` on `ctx.Done()`.
antipattern.reason: The `range` loop blocks until the channel is closed; if the context is cancelled first, the goroutine ignores it and keeps processing.

antipattern.description: Spawning `len(items)` goroutines unconditionally for a slice.
antipattern.reason: For large slices this creates thousands of goroutines simultaneously, exhausting memory and scheduler capacity. Use a bounded pool instead.
